Allow ext → ext dependency if triggers are a strict extensions","color":"c5def5","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-10-28T14:20:36Z","updated_at":"2024-10-28T23:38:11Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56368","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56368","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56368.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56368.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"This is an effort at a proper workaround (feature?) to resolve https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56204.

For example:
```toml
[extensions]
PlottingExt = "Plots"
StatisticsPlottingExt = ["Plots", "Statistics"]
```

Here `StatisticsPlottingExt` is allowed to depend on `PlottingExt` This provides a way to declare `ext → ext` dependencies while still avoiding any extension cycles. The same trick can also be used to make an extension in one package depend on an extension provided in another.

We'll also need to land #49891, so that we guarantee these load in the right order.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56368/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56368/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2442872666","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56368#issuecomment-2442872666","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56368","id":2442872666,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6Rm0da","user":{"login":"penelopeysm","id":122629585,"node_id":"U_kgDOB08t0Q","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/122629585?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/penelopeysm","html_url":"https://github.com/penelopeysm","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/penelopeysm/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/penelopeysm/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/penelopeysm/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/penelopeysm/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/penelopeysm/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/penelopeysm/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/penelopeysm/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/penelopeysm/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/penelopeysm/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-28T23:38:10Z","updated_at":"2024-10-28T23:38:10Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Hello! Having this feature would be really great, I just wanted to add though that for our usecase Bijectors.jl would have one of its strong deps as a dep of the extension, and so I understand the following Pkg.jl issue needs to be resolved so that we can get it to work on 1.10 as well (this issue doesn't happen on 1.11, I haven't yet looked into why):

https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/4018

(Re. our usecase: there's more context [here](https://github.com/TuringLang/Bijectors.jl/pull/333)), but TLDR we want `EnzymeChainRulesCoreExt` to load before `BijectorsEnzymeExt`. The former depends on `Enzyme` and `ChainRulesCore`, so with your PR we would be able to get that to load first by declaring the latter's deps to be `["Enzyme", "ChainRulesCore"]` – _but_ `ChainRulesCore` is itself a strong dep of `Bijectors`.)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2442872666/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
